content.name: write_todos
content.description:
Use this tool to create and manage a structured task list for your current work session. This helps you track progress, organize complex tasks, and demonstrate thoroughness to the user.

Only use this tool if you think it will be helpful in staying organized. If the user's request is trivial and takes less than 3 steps, it is better to NOT use this tool and just do the task directly.

## When to Use This Tool
Use this tool in these scenarios:

1. Complex multi-step tasks - When a task requires 3 or more distinct steps or actions
2. Non-trivial and complex tasks - Tasks that require careful planning or multiple operations
3. User explicitly requests todo list - When the user directly asks you to use the todo list
4. User provides multiple tasks - When users provide a list of things to be done (numbered or comma-separated)
5. The plan may need future revisions or updates based on results from the first few steps

## How to Use This Tool
1. When you start working on a task - Mark it as in_progress BEFORE beginning work.
2. After completing a task - Mark it as completed and add any new follow-up tasks discovered during implementation.
3. You can also update future tasks, such as deleting them if they are no longer necessary, or adding new tasks that are necessary. Don't change previously completed tasks.
4. You can make several updates to the todo list at once. For example, when you complete a task, you can mark the next task you need to start as in_progress.

## When NOT to Use This Tool
It is important to skip using this tool when:
1. There is only a single, straightforward task
2. The task is trivial and tracking it provides no benefit
3. The task can be completed in less than 3 trivial steps
4. The task is purely conversational or informational

## Task States and Management

1. **Task States**: Use these states to track progress:
   - pending: Task not yet started
   - in_progress: Currently working on (you can have multiple tasks in_progress at a time if they are not related to each other and can be run in parallel)
   - completed: Task finished successfully

2. **Task Management**:
   - Update task status in real-time as you work
   - Mark tasks complete IMMEDIATELY after finishing (don't batch completions)
   - Complete current tasks before starting new ones
   - Remove tasks that are no longer relevant from the list entirely
   - IMPORTANT: When you write this todo list, you should mark your first task (or tasks) as in_progress immediately!.
   - IMPORTANT: Unless all tasks are completed, you should always have at least one task in_progress to show the user that you are working on something.

3. **Task Completion Requirements**:
   - ONLY mark a task as completed when you have FULLY accomplished it
   - If you encounter errors, blockers, or cannot finish, keep the task as in_progress
   - When blocked, create a new task describing what needs to be resolved
   - Never mark a task as completed if:
     - There are unresolved issues or errors
     - Work is partial or incomplete
     - You encountered blockers that prevent completion
     - You couldn't find necessary resources or dependencies
     - Quality standards haven't been met

4. **Task Breakdown**:
   - Create specific, actionable items
   - Break complex tasks into smaller, manageable steps
   - Use clear, descriptive task names

Being proactive with task management demonstrates attentiveness and ensures you complete all requirements successfully
Remember: If you only need to make a few tool calls to complete a task, and it is clear what you need to do, it is better to just do the task directly and NOT call this tool at all.

items.description: A single todo item with content and status.

content.name: ls
content.description:
Lists all files in a directory.

This is useful for exploring the filesystem and finding the right file to read or edit.
You should almost ALWAYS use this tool before using the read_file or edit_file tools.

path.description: Absolute path to the directory to list. Must be absolute, not relative.

content.name: read_file
content.description:
Reads a file from the filesystem.

Assume this tool is able to read all files. If the User provides a path to a file assume that path is valid. It is okay to read a file that does not exist; an error will be returned.

Usage:
- By default, it reads up to 100 lines starting from the beginning of the file
- **IMPORTANT for large files and codebase exploration**: Use pagination with offset and limit parameters to avoid context overflow
  - First scan: read_file(path, limit=100) to see file structure
  - Read more sections: read_file(path, offset=100, limit=200) for next 200 lines
  - Only omit limit (read full file) when necessary for editing
- Specify offset and limit: read_file(path, offset=0, limit=100) reads first 100 lines
- Results are returned using cat -n format, with line numbers starting at 1
- Lines longer than 5,000 characters will be split into multiple lines with continuation markers (e.g., 5.1, 5.2, etc.). When you specify a limit, these continuation lines count towards the limit.
- You have the capability to call multiple tools in a single response. It is always better to speculatively read multiple files as a batch that are potentially useful.
- If you read a file that exists but has empty contents you will receive a system reminder warning in place of file contents.
- Image files (`.png`, `.jpg`, `.jpeg`, `.gif`, `.webp`, etc.), audio and video files, and PDFs are returned as multimodal content blocks (see https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/messages#multimodal).

For multimodal reads (image, audio, video, PDF, etc.):
- Use `read_file(file_path=...)`
- Do NOT use `offset`/`limit` for images (pagination is text-only)
- If file details were compacted from history, call `read_file` again on the same path

- You should ALWAYS make sure a file has been read before editing it.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the file to read. Must be absolute, not relative.

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (0-indexed). Use for pagination of large files.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read. Use for pagination of large files.

content.name: write_file
content.description:
Writes to a new file in the filesystem.

Usage:
- The write_file tool will create the a new file.
- Prefer to edit existing files (with the edit_file tool) over creating new ones when possible.

file_path.description: Absolute path where the file should be created. Must be absolute, not relative.

content.description: The text content to write to the file. This parameter is required.

content.name: edit_file
content.description:
Performs exact string replacements in files.

Usage:
- You must read the file before editing. This tool will error if you attempt an edit without reading the file first.
- When editing, preserve the exact indentation (tabs/spaces) from the read output. Never include line number prefixes in old_string or new_string.
- ALWAYS prefer editing existing files over creating new ones.
- Only use emojis if the user explicitly requests it.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the file to edit. Must be absolute, not relative.

old_string.description: The exact text to find and replace. Must be unique in the file unless replace_all is True.

new_string.description: The text to replace old_string with. Must be different from old_string.

replace_all.description: If True, replace all occurrences of old_string. If False (default), old_string must be unique.

content.name: glob
content.description:
Find files matching a glob pattern.

Supports standard glob patterns: `*` (any characters), `**` (any directories), `?` (single character).
Returns a list of absolute file paths that match the pattern.

Examples:
- `**/*.py` - Find all Python files
- `*.txt` - Find all text files in root
- `/subdir/**/*.md` - Find all markdown files under /subdir

path.description: Base directory to search from. Defaults to root '/'.

content.name: grep
content.description:
Search for a text pattern across files.

Searches for literal text (not regex) and returns matching files or content based on output_mode.
Special characters like parentheses, brackets, pipes, etc. are treated as literal characters, not regex operators.

Examples:
- Search all files: `grep(pattern="TODO")`
- Search Python files only: `grep(pattern="import", glob="*.py")`
- Show matching lines: `grep(pattern="error", output_mode="content")`
- Search for code with special chars: `grep(pattern="def __init__(self):")`

pattern.description: Text pattern to search for (literal string, not regex).

path.description: Directory to search in. Defaults to current working directory.

glob.description: Glob pattern to filter which files to search (e.g., '*.py').

output_mode.description: Output format: 'files_with_matches' (file paths only, default), 'content' (matching lines with context), 'count' (match counts per file).

content.name: remember
content.description: Append a user-requested memory to /memory/HABITS.md with a timestamp. Provide `content` as the memory text.

content.name: add_attachments
content.description: Share files (images, videos, audio, documents) with the user. Use for individual shots, segments, re-generated clips, audio, images, or any reference material. Also use when showing multiple files that are not mutually exclusive alternatives. Do not use for assembled videos (use show_final_video) or creative-direction choices (use show_preview_videos). Pass canonical /projects/{project_id}/workspace/assets/... paths or legacy /workspace/assets/... paths.

file_paths.description: List of virtual file paths to attach, e.g. /projects/{project_id}/workspace/assets/xxx or legacy /workspace/assets/xxx

content.name: show_final_video
content.description: Deliver an assembled video representing the current version of the project. Use when you have composed a video from segments (with or without audio) and are presenting it as a complete viewing experience. Do not use for individual shots or segments. Pass a single canonical /projects/{project_id}/workspace/assets/... path or legacy /workspace/assets/... path.

file_path.description: Virtual file path of the final video to display, e.g. /projects/{project_id}/workspace/assets/xxx or legacy /workspace/assets/xxx

content.name: show_preview_videos
content.description: Present 2+ videos as alternatives for the user to choose between. Use only when the options are mutually exclusive and the user needs to select one direction. Do not use for showing segments that will all be used, or for assembled results. Pass canonical /projects/{project_id}/workspace/assets/... paths or legacy /workspace/assets/... paths.

file_paths.description: List of virtual file paths for preview videos. Provide 2+ videos for user to compare and choose. Use /projects/{project_id}/workspace/assets/xxx or legacy /workspace/assets/xxx.

content.name: analyze_file_content
content.description: Use Gemini to analyze image, video, or audio file content (multimodal understanding). Only supports image/*, video/*, audio/* MIME types. Pass a canonical /projects/{project_id}/workspace/assets/... path or legacy /workspace/assets/... path and a query describing what to analyze.

file_path.description: Virtual file path, e.g. /projects/{project_id}/workspace/assets/xxx or legacy /workspace/assets/xxx

query.description: Question or prompt to ask Gemini for analyzing the file content

content.name: summarize_url
content.description: Read and summarize (or answer questions about) the content at a single given URL. Requires a 'query' (e.g. 'summarize', 'list main points'). Use when you have a specific webpage URL. Does NOT search the web; only processes the provided URL.

url.description: The URL of the webpage to read and summarize

query.description: Instruction for how to process the content (e.g. summarize, list main points). Required.

model_name.description: Gemini model to use for content understanding

content.name: get_file_info
content.description: Get file information by virtual path. Returns: file_name, file_size, file_format, signed_url. Pass a canonical /projects/{project_id}/workspace/assets/... path or legacy /workspace/assets/... path.

file_path.description: File path, e.g. /projects/{project_id}/workspace/assets/xxx_image.png or legacy /workspace/assets/xxx_image.png

content.name: video-editor__analyze_audio
content.description: Analyze an audio (or video) file and return its duration in seconds, integrated loudness in LUFS (EBU R128), and detected tempo in BPM. Use this before editing to understand source material loudness and timing.

file.description: File reference for the audio to analyze (e.g. asset://a_xxx).

content.name: video-editor__execute_edit_video
content.description: Execute video editing. Accepts a DSL object describing output settings and tracks, renders the result, and returns the output file reference.

name.description: Optional job name used only for debugging and logs.

fps.description: Output frames per second. Use 1 for single-frame PNG export.

format.description: Output format. Use 'mp4' for video or 'png' for a single still image; PNG requires exactly one rendered frame.

target_lufs.description: Master output loudness target in LUFS (e.g. -14.0). Applied after all tracks are mixed using two-pass linear loudnorm. Omit to skip master normalization.

kind.description: Track kind: video, audio, text, subtitle, or overlay.

source_type.description: Clip source type. Omit or use 'file' for file input; use 'color' to generate a solid-color clip.

color.description: Solid color value for source_type=color, for example '#000000'.

speed.description: Playback speed multiplier. 2.0 is 2x faster; 0.5 is half speed.

x.description: Canvas X offset in pixels for overlay placement.

y.description: Canvas Y offset in pixels for overlay placement.

width.description: Rendered clip width in pixels after layout scaling.

height.description: Rendered clip height in pixels after layout scaling.

scale.description: Uniform scale multiplier relative to the natural or layout size.

z.description: Z order for overlays; higher values render on top.

x.description: Crop left offset in pixels.

y.description: Crop top offset in pixels.

crop.description: Crop rectangle applied before scaling and placement.

volume.description: Linear volume multiplier for this clip.

dbfs.description: Target loudness adjustment in dBFS for this clip.

font.description: Font family id returned by list_fonts.

font_weight.description: Optional font weight. Prefer numeric values like '400', '500', '700'.

fontsize.description: Font size in pixels for drawtext fontsize=.

fontcolor.description: Text color for drawtext fontcolor= (named color or #RRGGBB).

x.description: Horizontal anchor for drawtext x=. Use FFmpeg expressions, e.g. (w-text_w)/2 to center, or a pixel offset string like 100.

y.description: Vertical anchor for drawtext y=. Use FFmpeg expressions, e.g. h-100 to place text near the bottom, or (h-text_h)/2 for vertical center.

borderw.description: Outline width in pixels for drawtext borderw=.

bordercolor.description: Outline color for drawtext bordercolor= (named or hex).

shadowx.description: Shadow X offset in pixels (drawtext shadowx=). Typically set together with shadowy.

shadowy.description: Shadow Y offset in pixels (drawtext shadowy=). Typically set together with shadowx.

shadowcolor.description: Shadow color for drawtext shadowcolor= (named or hex).

box.description: Enable text background box: set to '1' to enable.

boxcolor.description: Background box color for drawtext boxcolor= (e.g. '#000000@0.55').

boxborderw.description: Padding around text inside the background box for drawtext boxborderw=.

alignment.description: ASS numpad alignment for subtitle tracks (1-9). 2=bottom-center (default), 5=center, 8=top-center.

margin_v.description: Vertical margin in pixels for subtitle tracks (distance from edge). Default 24.

text_opts.description: Text styling and position options for text or subtitle rendering.

file.description: Input file reference string. Required for file-backed video/audio/subtitle/overlay clips.

fps.description: Override source FPS when input metadata is missing or wrong.

in_ms.description: Inclusive source-local start time in milliseconds; not a timeline position. Defaults to 0.

out_ms.description: Exclusive source-local end time in milliseconds; not a timeline position. Clip duration is out_ms - in_ms.

source.description: File-backed source reference and source-local trim. Omit for text or source_type=color clips.

start_ms.description: Timeline placement start time in milliseconds. Omit for sequential placement after the previous clip on the same track. Timeline end = start_ms + (out_ms - in_ms), after speed.

end_ms.description: Timeline placement end time in milliseconds. Use for text/subtitle/overlay and source_type=color clips. File-backed video/audio clips get duration from source.out_ms instead.

e.description: Interpolation easing from this keyframe to the next one.

ms.description: Time in milliseconds relative to the start of the clip.

scale_kf.description: Scale keyframes applied over the clip duration.

e.description: Interpolation easing from this keyframe to the next one.

ms.description: Time in milliseconds relative to the start of the clip.

speed_kf.description: Playback speed keyframes applied over the clip duration.

e.description: Interpolation easing from this keyframe to the next one.

ms.description: Time in milliseconds relative to the start of the clip.

opacity_kf.description: Opacity keyframes applied over the clip duration.

e.description: Interpolation easing from this keyframe to the next one.

ms.description: Time in milliseconds relative to the start of the clip.

e.description: Interpolation easing from this keyframe to the next one.

ms.description: Time in milliseconds relative to the start of the clip.

clips.description: Clips on this track. For video/audio tracks they play in order unless start_ms is set.

in_ms.description: Incoming transition duration in milliseconds when in/out lengths differ.

out_ms.description: Outgoing transition duration in milliseconds when in/out lengths differ.

transitions.description: Transitions between adjacent clips on a video track. Expected length is len(clips)-1.

tracks.description: Timeline tracks. Video/audio clips are sequential within a track; text, subtitle, and overlay clips can be placed with start_ms.

content.name: video-editor__ffprobe
content.description: Probe a media file and return format/stream information using ffprobe. The file parameter should be a file reference string.

extra_args.description: Optional extra ffprobe arguments as a JSON array of strings.

file.description: File reference for the media to inspect.

content.name: video-editor__list_fonts
content.description: List all available preset font families. Returns each family's id (use this as text_opts.font), display name, supported languages, available weights, and styles.

content.name: audio_produce
content.description:
Unified audio generation tool.

Hard rules:
1) Top-level fields provider/mode/model are required.
2) provider_param must contain exactly one capability block key.
3) Do not place provider/mode/model in provider_param.
4) Inline audio tags such as [excited] and [whispers] are supported in text.

mode.description: Generation mode. Must match the selected provider_param block.

model.description: Provider model identifier. Must match the selected provider_param block.

name.description: Asset name used when uploading. 1-30 chars, letters/digits/underscore/hyphen only (regex: ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,30}$).

provider.description: Provider identifier. Must match the selected provider_param block.

provider_param.description: Required. Provider-specific parameters only (do not include name/provider/mode/model here). Pass exactly one block key. Allowed keys: elevenlabs_text2speech, elevenlabs_speech2text.

elevenlabs_speech2text.description: ElevenLabs speech-to-text (scribe_v2). Server-managed: model_id, timestamps_granularity=character, diarize=false, file_format=other, enable_logging=true. Only for provider=elevenlabs, mode=speech2text, model in [scribe_v2].

file.description: Audio or video file to transcribe. Accepts asset:// references or HTTPS URLs.

no_verbatim.description: Remove filler words (e.g. um, uh) from the transcript. Default: false. Requires scribe_v2.

tag_audio_events.description: Detect non-speech audio events such as laughter or applause. Default: false.

elevenlabs_text2speech.description: ElevenLabs text-to-speech (v3) with character-level timestamp alignment. Server-managed: model_id=eleven_v3, output_format=mp3_44100_128, apply_text_normalization=auto. Only for provider=elevenlabs, mode=text2speech, model in [eleven_v3].

language_code.description: ISO 639-1 language code (e.g. en, zh, ja, ko, fr, de). When omitted, the model auto-detects from text. Recommended for non-English or mixed-language content.

seed.description: Deterministic seed, 0-4294967295. Same seed with identical parameters produces approximately the same output.

similarity_boost.description: Adherence to the original voice timbre, 0.0-1.0. Higher values replicate the reference voice more closely.

speed.description: Speech rate multiplier, 0.5-2.0. 1.0 is normal speed.

stability.description: Voice consistency, 0.0-1.0. Lower values produce more expressive variation; higher values produce steadier, more monotone output.

text.description: Text to convert to speech. Supports inline audio tags for emotion control in lowercase brackets: [excited], [whispers], [sad], [laughs], [sighs], [shouts]. Tags can precede a sentence or appear mid-sentence, e.g. '[nervous] Is anyone there? [whispers] I thought I heard something...'

voice_id.description: ElevenLabs voice identifier from the preset voice catalog.

content.name: image_generate
content.description:
Unified image generation tool.

Model guidance:
- Use gemini when you need consistency-preserving reference workflows, realistic rendering, image-to-image iteration, or packaging/key-frame generation. Prefer it when a reliable visual reference already exists and the goal is to extend, adapt, or refine it while keeping the result stable.
- Use seedream when you need high-aesthetic hero visuals, fine-detail rendering, polished brand/product imagery, or a more visually finished result from either text or image input.
- Use fal (model=openai/gpt-image-2) when you need fine-grained natural language edits to existing images, in-painting, targeted region edits (optionally with a mask), or text-heavy illustrations requiring fine typography. For image2image, it delivers the most precise prompt-driven edits.

Hard rules:
1) Top-level fields provider/mode/model are required.
2) provider_param must contain exactly one capability block key.
3) Do not place provider/mode/model in provider_param.
4) Unknown fields inside provider_param are rejected.
5) Do not mix video/music-only fields into image blocks (for example duration, duration_seconds, aspect_ratio for non-supported models, or music_length_ms).

mode.description: Generation mode. Must match the selected provider_param block.

model.description: Provider model identifier. Must match the selected provider_param block.

name.description: Asset name used when uploading. 1-30 chars, letters/digits/underscore/hyphen only (regex: ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,30}$).

provider.description: Provider identifier. Must match the selected provider_param block.

provider_param.description: Required. Provider-specific parameters only (do not include name/provider/mode/model here). Pass exactly one block key. Allowed keys: seedream_text2image, seedream_image2image, gemini_text2image, gemini_image2image, fal_gptimage2_text2image, fal_gptimage2_image2image.

fal_gptimage2_image2image.description: Only for provider=fal, mode=image2image, model in [openai/gpt-image-2]. Edit an existing image via OpenAI GPT Image 2 on FAL. Best for fine-grained edits, style changes, in-painting, and targeted modifications using natural language. Supports optional mask for precise region control.

aspect_ratio.description: Optional. Output aspect ratio for the edited image. If omitted, defaults to 4:3 and sends a custom FAL image_size of 2304x1728.

files.description: Reference image paths as provided by the runtime. Always pass an array, including single-image edits. Maximum 14 images.

mask_url.description: Optional. URL of a mask image (white=edit region, black=preserve). Must match source image dimensions.

prompt.description: Editing instruction describing the desired change. For multiple reference images, identify each role by order, for example image 1, image 2.

fal_gptimage2_text2image.description: Only for provider=fal, mode=text2image, model in [openai/gpt-image-2]. Generate image via OpenAI GPT Image 2 on FAL. Best for detailed images with fine typography, product mockups, and high-fidelity illustration. Supports up to 4K resolution with flexible aspect ratios.

aspect_ratio.description: Output aspect ratio. Maps to custom FAL image_size {width,height}: 1:1=2048x2048, 4:3=2304x1728, 3:4=1728x2304, 16:9=2848x1600, 9:16=1600x2848. Default 4:3.

prompt.description: Image generation prompt. Detailed descriptions yield better results.

gemini_image2image.description: Only for provider=gemini, mode=image2image, model in [gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview]. Generate image via Google Gemini / Nano Banana 2 (Gemini 3.1 Flash Image). Best for consistency-preserving reference workflows, realistic rendering, image-to-image iteration, and packaging/key-frame generation. Use when a reliable visual reference already exists and the goal is to extend, adapt, or refine it while keeping the result stable. Especially strong for editing or extending an existing image without drifting too far from the source.

aspect_ratio.description: Output aspect ratio. Gemini receives this value directly with image_size fixed to 2K. Default 4:3.

files.description: Reference image paths as provided by the runtime, pointing at images (not video/audio). Always pass an array, including single-image edits. Maximum 14 images.

prompt.description: Gemini image editing prompt. Describe exactly how to transform the source images. For multiple references, identify each role by order, for example image 1, image 2. Gemini supports up to 14 images; official guidance is up to 10 object/detail references plus up to 4 character images.

gemini_text2image.description: Only for provider=gemini, mode=text2image, model in [gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview]. Generate image via Google Gemini / Nano Banana 2 (Gemini 3.1 Flash Image). Best for consistency-preserving reference workflows, realistic rendering, image-to-image iteration, and packaging/key-frame generation. Use when a reliable visual reference already exists and the goal is to extend, adapt, or refine it while keeping the result stable. Also suitable for text-to-image tasks that benefit from strong composition and text rendering.

aspect_ratio.description: Output aspect ratio. Gemini receives this value directly with image_size fixed to 2K. Default 4:3.

prompt.description: Prompt for image generation.

seedream_image2image.description: Only for provider=seedream, mode=image2image, model in [seedream-4-5-251128]. Generate image via Volcengine Seedream 4.5. Best for high-aesthetic hero visuals, fine-detail rendering, polished brand/product imagery, and high-quality text-to-image or image-to-image generation. Use when the goal is a more refined, delicate, and visually finished result, whether starting from text or upgrading an existing visual reference. Especially suitable when an existing image needs to be elevated into a more polished final visual.

files.description: Reference image paths as provided by the runtime. Always pass an array, including single-image edits. Maximum 14 images.

guidance_scale.description: Guidance scale (optional). Must be between 1.0 and 10.0; recommended around 2.5 for subtle edits. Do not send values outside this range.

prompt.description: Describe the desired edits in detail. For multiple reference images, refer to each role by order, for example image 1, image 2.

response_format.description: Response format. url=direct image URL, b64_json=base64 image data.

seedream_text2image.description: Only for provider=seedream, mode=text2image, model in [seedream-4-5-251128]. Generate image via Volcengine Seedream 4.5. Best for high-aesthetic hero visuals, fine-detail rendering, polished brand/product imagery, and high-quality text-to-image or image-to-image generation. Use when the goal is a more refined, delicate, and visually finished result, whether starting from text or upgrading an existing visual reference.

prompt.description: Describe the target image in detail, max 2500 chars.

response_format.description: Response format. url=direct image URL, b64_json=base64 image data.

content.name: music_generate
content.description:
Unified music generation tool.

Hard rules:
1) Top-level fields provider/mode/model are required.
2) provider_param must contain exactly one capability block key.
3) Do not place provider/mode/model in provider_param.
4) Unknown fields inside provider_param are rejected.
5) Do not mix image/video-only fields into music blocks (for example size, resolution, aspect_ratio, duration, or duration_seconds).

mode.description: Generation mode. Must match the selected provider_param block.

model.description: Provider model identifier. Must match the selected provider_param block.

name.description: Asset name used when uploading. 1-30 chars, letters/digits/underscore/hyphen only (regex: ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,30}$).

provider.description: Provider identifier. Must match the selected provider_param block.

provider_param.description: Required. Provider-specific parameters only (do not include name/provider/mode/model here). Pass exactly one block key. Allowed keys: elevenlabs_text2music.

elevenlabs_text2music.description: Only for provider=elevenlabs, mode=text2music, model in [music_v1].

composition_plan.description: Structured composition plan with styles and sections. Exactly one of `prompt` or `composition_plan` must be set.

negative_global_styles.description: Free-form style tags to avoid across the whole song.

positive_global_styles.description: Free-form style tags to include across the whole song.

sections.description: Ordered song sections with per-section styles, duration, and optional lyrics.

lines.description: Lyrics lines for this section. For non-instrumental generation, this field is required and should contain at least one line (use [""] as a placeholder if needed). For instrumental generation, use [].

negative_local_styles.description: Free-form style tags to avoid in this section, for example ['slow tempo', 'acoustic guitar'].

positive_local_styles.description: Free-form style tags to include in this section, for example ['upbeat pop', 'electronic drums'].

section_name.description: Section label such as intro, verse, chorus, bridge, or outro.

duration_ms.description: Requested length in milliseconds. Valid range is 10000-300000 (for example 30s = 30000). Use `duration_ms` in this schema block.

force_instrumental.description: Generate instrumental output only. CANNOT be used with `composition_plan`, only be used with `prompt`.

prompt.description: High-level music prompt text. Exactly one of `prompt` or `composition_plan` must be set.

content.name: video_generate
content.description:
Unified video generation tool.

Hard rules:
1) Top-level fields provider/mode/model are required.
2) provider_param must contain exactly one capability block key.
3) Do not place provider/mode/model in provider_param.
4) Unknown fields inside provider_param are rejected.
5) Do not mix image/music-only fields into video blocks (for example size, seed, music_length_ms, or output_format).

mode.description: Generation mode. Must match the selected provider_param block.

model.description: Provider model identifier. Must match the selected provider_param block.

name.description: Asset name used when uploading. 1-30 chars, letters/digits/underscore/hyphen only (regex: ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,30}$).

provider.description: Provider identifier. Must match the selected provider_param block.

provider_param.description: Required. Provider-specific parameters only (do not include name/provider/mode/model here). Pass exactly one block key. Allowed keys: kling_reference2video, fal_grok_text2video, seedance_reference2video.

fal_grok_text2video.description: Only for provider=fal, mode=text2video, model in [xai/grok-imagine-video/text-to-video].

aspect_ratio.description: Aspect ratio, for example 16:9, 9:16, or 1:1. Pass a provider-supported ratio when provided.

duration_seconds.description: Duration in seconds. Must be between 1 and 15 when provided.

prompt.description: Prompt for Grok text-to-video. Do not send image/audio-only fields in this block.

resolution.description: Resolution. Must be exactly 480p or 720p when provided; 1080p is not supported.

kling_reference2video.description: Only for provider=kling, mode=reference2video, model in [kling-v3-omni].

aspect_ratio.description: Output aspect ratio. Required for create video. For edit video (refer_type=base), it can be omitted and output follows the input video's ratio. If video_list is provided with refer_type=feature, aspect_ratio is still required.

duration.description: Output duration in seconds. Without video_list: 3-15. With video_list and refer_type=feature: 3-10 only. With refer_type=base: ignored, because output duration follows the input video duration.

image_list.description: Reference images for guiding visual direction. Every image must have a clear role declaration in the prompt (for example product reference, character reference, style reference). Capacity rules: up to 7 images when video_list is empty; up to 4 images when video_list is also provided.

file.description: Path to a reference image as provided by the runtime. Prefer original JPG/PNG (not thumbnails), at least 300x300.

prompt.description: Free-form storyboard prompt. For create video (refer_type=feature or no video_list): explicitly map every reference asset to its role, for example 'image 1 provides the character', 'image 2 provides the style', 'video 1 provides the camera motion'. Then describe subject actions, camera movement, timing cues, atmosphere, and SFX direction. For edit video (refer_type=base): describe what to change and what style to apply while preserving the input video's structure. Do not put media references inside the prompt; use image_list/video_list item file fields.

sound.description: Audio co-generation switch. Must be 'off' whenever video_list is provided. Use 'on' only for create-video requests without video input. When 'on', synchronized SFX can be generated; for a single continuous shot <=15s, music/dialogue may also be co-generated. For multi-shot sequences, only SFX is reliable; music and dialogue should usually be handled in post.

video_list.description: Reference or edit video input. At most 1 item per call. When provided, sound must be off, audio co-generation is unavailable, and image_list capacity drops to 4.

file.description: Path to reference or edit video (mp4/mov) as provided by the runtime. At most 1 item; input should be <=10s.

keep_original_sound.description: Preserve the input video's original audio. Only meaningful for edit video (refer_type=base); usually omit it for feature mode.

refer_type.description: Usage scenario. 'feature': create a new video while borrowing motion/camera/composition from the input video; duration must be 3-10 and sound must be off. 'base': edit the input video's visuals while preserving its structure/timing/motion; duration follows the input video, aspect_ratio can be omitted, and sound must be off.

seedance_reference2video.description: Only for provider=seedance, mode=reference2video, model in [doubao-seedance-2-0-260128, doubao-seedance-2-0-fast-260128].

aspect_ratio.description: Output aspect ratio. Required.

audio_list.description: Reference audio clips. Up to 3 items; each clip 2–15 s, combined duration of all clips ≤ 15 s. Cannot be the sole input — at least one image or video must also be provided. Distinct from sound (on/off), which controls generated audio in the output video. Total reference items across image_list + video_list + audio_list must not exceed 12.

file.description: Path to a reference audio clip as provided by the runtime.

duration.description: Output video duration in whole seconds. Required. Must be 4–15 (string digits). Do not use -1 or omit.

image_list.description: Reference images. Up to 9 items. Total reference items across image_list + video_list + audio_list must not exceed 12.

file.description: Path to a reference image as provided by the runtime.

prompt.description: Text prompt for video generation. Supports Chinese and English; max ~500 Chinese characters or ~1000 English words. Can reference uploaded assets by position (e.g. 'image 1', 'video 2'). Double-quoted text is interpreted as dialogue for speech synthesis when sound is on. At least one of prompt, image_list, video_list, or audio_list is required; audio_list alone is not valid. Never place media references in prompt — use image_list/video_list/audio_list item file fields instead.

sound.description: Controls whether the output video has generated audio. 'on' (default): model generates voice, SFX, and BGM based on prompt and visuals; output is mono. 'off': silent video. Distinct from audio_list, which provides reference audio clips as input.

video_list.description: Reference video clips. Up to 3 items; each clip 2–15 s, combined duration of all clips ≤ 15 s. Total reference items across image_list + video_list + audio_list must not exceed 12.

file.description: Path to a reference video as provided by the runtime.

content.text:
我来为你规划5个差异化的风格方向，然后同步生成：

1. **极简日式禅意** — 留白、水墨、克制美学
2. **复古欧洲工坊** — 铜版刻字、皮革棕、手工质感
3. **现代都市潮流** — 几何图形、高对比、当代品牌感
4. **自然有机田园** — 草木插画、大地色、可持续调性
5. **深夜奢华暗调** — 黑金配色、烟雾感、沉浸体验
